wordmark.purpose: Lexend ExtraBold (800). The lockup + small top-bar wordmark.

display.purpose: Lexend 800, uppercase. Page headlines (BUILD ON THIRD WAVE.) — shares the wordmark's face.

lede.purpose: Schibsted 400 ~22px. Hero tagline and ledes — the narrator voice (no longer a serif).

label.purpose: Schibsted 600–700, uppercase, tracked 0.14em. Nav, CTAs, ticker, pills, metadata.

shadows.name: status-glow
shadows.purpose: The 6x6 live-status dot ONLY. The brand's sole box-shadow.

motion.name: hover
motion.purpose: Color/background hover transitions. No scale, no translate (tiles excepted).

components.name: Primary Button (inverse fill)
components.description: Email submit / primary action. White fill, black text, square.

components.name: Outline Button (header CTA)
components.description: Header CTA. Transparent with 1px white border; inverts on hover.

components.name: Email Field
components.description: Transparent input inside a 1px hairline group, square.

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Uppercase tracked label, dim → bright on hover/active.

components.name: Ticker
components.description: Inverted full-bleed marquee band — the site's heartbeat.
components.html: A New Medium · 100 Players · One Screen · Not a Game, Not a Movie · No Goggles ·

components.name: Live-Status Pill
components.description: Glowing pulsing dot + tracked status label.

narrative.overview: The system frames one asset: the pure-white knockout lockup. Text is #ffffff (not off-white) to match it; the chassis is a barely-warm off-black #0a0a0a, the only off-endpoint. Theater-marquee meets developer terminal — nearly-black house lights, off-white type, one bright element per screen. Lexend is the display voice (wordmark + headlines, sharing one face so they never clash) — a deliberately low-opinion pick for a young brand; Schibsted Grotesk is the text voice (body, ledes, labels). Chosen off the AI reflex-reject defaults (no Inter, no Newsreader, no editorial-serif lane). Louder personality is carried by copy, motion, and texture, not the type identity. The homepage is image-led: a heavily-treated B&W photographic hero (modern-streetwear register, Cav Empt / Brain Dead) carries the edge while the type stays a quiet chassis — quiet chassis, loud content.
keyCharacteristics: Off-black chassis (#0a0a0a), pure-white type and CTAs (#ffffff) — the lockup sets the spec.
Zero radii everywhere; only circles are the status dot and play button.
1px hairline rules carry all structure; no shadow system.
One bright thing per screen. Color lives inside content frames, never the chassis.
Two type roles by size/job: Lexend = display (wordmark + headlines); Schibsted Grotesk = text (body, ledes, labels).
The homepage is image-led: a heavily-treated B&W hero plate carries the edge; type stays quiet over it. Subpages keep the chassis without the plate.

rules.name: The One Bright Thing Rule
rules.body: At most one loud element per screen — ticker, live pill, or white CTA. Quiet the UI around a loud content tile.

rules.name: The No Accent Rule
rules.body: No chromatic brand accent. Inversion (white↔black) is the only accent.

rules.name: The Color-In-Content Rule
rules.body: Saturation belongs to genuine show imagery and venue photos, never the chassis. Don't desaturate genuine content; don't tint the chassis.

rules.name: The Treated-Imagery Rule
rules.body: The brand's own marketing plates (homepage hero, section graphics) are deliberately heavy B&W treatments (high-contrast, grain, streetwear print register) — that treatment IS the aesthetic, the loud content the quiet chassis frames. Distinct from genuine show/venue imagery (which keeps its saturation). Bake the treatment into the asset, not runtime CSS filters, so scroll stays GPU-smooth.

rules.name: The Display-Is-Lexend Rule
rules.body: Lexend sets the wordmark AND every headline — and only those. Sharing one face across mark and headline keeps them from clashing. Body/ledes/labels are Schibsted; a headline in Schibsted is wrong.

rules.name: The Low-Opinion-Display Rule
rules.body: Keep the display face neutral and flexible while the brand is young. Don't swap Lexend for a charactered display as a shortcut to personality — that's an identity bet you can't cheaply undo. Personality belongs in copy, motion, texture.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces never lift. Use a surface-ladder step or a hairline rule, not a box-shadow. The only shadow is the status-dot glow.

dos: Do keep the chassis strictly monochrome.
Do let exactly one bright thing live per screen.
Do carry structure with 1px hairline rules and the surface ladder, not shadows.
Do use Lexend for display (wordmark + headlines) and Schibsted for text (body, ledes, uppercase tracked labels/CTAs). One face across mark + headline prevents clashing.
Do let genuine content imagery be as saturated as the source.
Do treat the brand's own hero/marketing plates heavily (high-contrast B&W, grain, streetwear print) — bake the treatment into the asset, not runtime CSS filters.
Do ship a prefers-reduced-motion alternative for every animation.
donts: Don't look like loud arcade/gamer, generic SaaS, web3/crypto, or corporate/enterprise.
Don't introduce a chromatic brand accent.
Don't add box-shadow, backdrop-filter, blur, or translucent layers (status-dot glow excepted).
Don't use decorative monospace; labels are tracked Schibsted.
Don't reintroduce Inter or Newsreader, or an editorial display-serif-italic headline.
Don't set body/ledes/labels in Lexend or headlines in Schibsted; don't swap Lexend for a charactered display face as a personality shortcut while the brand is young.
Don't desaturate content imagery or tint the chassis to decorate.
Don't use the editorial saturated darks as default chassis, cards, or rules.
Don't use exclamation marks or emoji in product copy.
